article_title: Agios Submits Oral Mitapivat for FDA Accelerated Approval in Sickle Cell as RISE UP Phase 3 Data Heads to EHA 2026 Plenary

content_preview.title: Agios Submits Oral Mitapivat for FDA Accelerated Approval in Sickle Cell as RISE UP Phase 3 Data Heads to EHA 2026 Plenary
content_preview.summary: Agios filed for accelerated approval of mitapivat, an oral pyruvate kinase activator, on the strength of a Phase 3 trial that met its hemoglobin endpoint but missed on pain crises.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Agios Pharmaceuticals has submitted a supplemental New Drug Application to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration seeking accelerated approval of mitapivat, an oral pyruvate kinase activator, for the treatment of sickle cell disease. The company announced the filing on [May 12, 2026](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/05/12/3293011/31990/en/Agios-Submits-sNDA-to-FDA-for-U-S-Accelerated-Approval-of-Mitapivat-in-Sickle-Cell-Disease.html). The application rests on the Phase...

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent, carefully-hedged clinical News piece (800 words) that foregrounds the mixed RISE UP result (hemoglobin endpoint met, pain-crisis co-primary missed) and the accelerated-approval pathway, with model Rule-9 stat attribution.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 4 snapshots read from disk (HTTP 200). source-2 (November 2025 topline release) carries — verbatim — every exact statistic: 40.6% vs 2.9% hemoglobin response (≥1.0 g/dL, Week 24-52), p<0.0001, the 7.69 vs 0.26 g/L hemoglobin change, the -16.03 vs 0.88 µmol/L indirect-bilirubin change, the 2.62 vs 3.05 annualized pain-crisis rate with p=0.1213, and the 'consistent with … prior mitapivat sickle cell disease trials' safety line. source-0 (May 12 sNDA release) confirms the 207-patient 2:1 (138/69) design, the 'oral mitapivat (100 mg) twice daily' dosing, and the PK-activator description. source-3 (BioSpace) confirms the ~159-patient confirmatory trial (age 12+), the 'transfusion free from Week 4 through Week 52' endpoint, the Q3-2026 acceptance / 60-day review, the 'first PK activator approved in the U.S.' claim, and the Sarah Gheuens quote. source-1 (EHA release) confirms the two primary endpoints, the Stockholm June 11-14 congress, the 'one of only six selected' plenary detail, and the June 13 9:00 a.m. ET call.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Exemplary Rule-9 discipline: every exact percentage and p-value is cited to the November 2025 topline release that actually contains it, NOT the May sNDA release that omits them. Every quote is verbatim and correctly attributed; minor renderings ('(100 mg) twice daily' → 'at 100 mg twice daily', 'transfusion free' → 'transfusion-free') are faithful. The internal cross-reference to the April Editas CRISPR sickle-cell article was verified to exist.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and precise; clearly states the pain-crisis endpoint missed significance and that the confirmatory-trial benefit lies years ahead.
editor_notes.originality: New topic — distinct oral PK-activator drug, contrasted with the archive's CRISPR/gene-therapy sickle-cell coverage.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, fully-verified clinical submission with model primary-source statistic attribution. APPROVE.
